title: Yandex tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Yandex OAuth/API token detected. This token provides authenticated access to various Yandex services including authentication, user data, cloud services, and platform APIs. Unauthorized access can lead to account compromise, data breach, service abuse, and potential identity theft. Immediately revoke this token from your Yandex OAuth applications or service console and rotate credentials using secure environment variables.

Yandex long-format token detected. This extended token grants comprehensive access to Yandex services with potentially elevated permissions including user account access, cloud resources, and service integrations. Exposing long Yandex tokens enables full account takeover, data exfiltration, and unauthorized service usage. Revoke immediately from your Yandex account security settings and implement secure credential storage.

Yandex client token or session identifier detected. These shorter tokens are used for client authentication, session management, and service identification within Yandex ecosystem. While potentially less sensitive than full access tokens, exposing these credentials can still lead to session hijacking, service abuse, and unauthorized access to user data. Store securely using environment variables or secure configuration management.
